item.name: Chrono Trigger
item.reasoning: Routinely ranked among the greatest RPGs of all time and topped multiple Famitsu all-time reader polls; its 1995 SNES release is a landmark, and the DS re-release holds a 92 on Metacritic.
item.subtitle: 1995 / Square / JRPG

item.name: Grand Theft Auto: San Andreas
item.reasoning: Holds a 95 Metacritic score and is the best-selling PlayStation 2 game with over 27 million copies sold, long cited among the greatest games ever made.
item.subtitle: 2004 / Rockstar / Action

item.name: God of War (2018)
item.reasoning: Won The Game Awards 2018 Game of the Year and holds a 94 Metacritic score, praised for reinventing the franchise, with over 23 million copies sold.
item.subtitle: 2018 / Santa Monica / Action

item.name: Dark Souls
item.reasoning: Codified and popularized the 'Soulslike' genre and holds an 89 Metacritic score, routinely appearing on greatest-games lists for its influential difficulty and interconnected world design.
item.subtitle: 2011 / FromSoftware / Action RPG

item.name: Sonic the Hedgehog (2006)
item.reasoning: Widely regarded as one of the worst entries in a major franchise, holding a 46 Metacritic score and infamous for game-breaking bugs and broken physics at launch.
item.subtitle: 2006 / Sonic Team / Platformer

item.name: PlayStation
item.subtitle: 1994 / Sony
item.reasoning: The original PlayStation was the first home console to ship over 100 million units and drove the industry's shift to 3D graphics on CD-ROM media.

item.name: PlayStation 5
item.subtitle: 2020 / Sony
item.reasoning: The PS5 is the best-selling ninth-generation home console, surpassing 65 million units within roughly three years of its 2020 launch.

item.name: Nintendo DS
item.subtitle: 2004 / Nintendo
item.reasoning: With over 154 million units sold, the Nintendo DS family is one of the best-selling gaming platforms of all time, second only to the PlayStation 2 among dedicated game systems.

item.name: Nintendo Entertainment System
item.subtitle: 1985 / Nintendo
item.reasoning: The NES revived the North American video game market after the 1983 crash and sold roughly 62 million units worldwide, establishing Nintendo's industry dominance.

item.name: Nintendo 64
item.subtitle: 1996 / Nintendo
item.reasoning: The Nintendo 64 popularized the analog control stick for 3D movement and launched critically acclaimed genre-definers like Super Mario 64 and The Legend of Zelda: Ocarina of Time.

item.name: Sega Genesis
item.subtitle: 1988 / Sega
item.reasoning: The Sega Genesis sold roughly 30-35 million units and, led by Sonic the Hedgehog and aggressive marketing, made the 16-bit console war genuinely competitive against Nintendo.

item.name: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King
item.reasoning: Won all 11 Academy Awards it was nominated for in 2004, including Best Picture, tying Ben-Hur and Titanic for the most Oscars ever won by a single film.
item.subtitle: 2003 / Peter Jackson / epic fantasy

art.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Return of the King

item.name: Goodfellas
item.reasoning: Holds a 95%+ critics score on Rotten Tomatoes and was selected for preservation in the U.S. National Film Registry as a landmark of the gangster genre.
item.subtitle: 1990 / Martin Scorsese / crime

item.name: Forrest Gump
item.reasoning: Won six Academy Awards including Best Picture, Best Director, and Best Actor, and grossed over $678 million worldwide to become a defining cultural touchstone of the 1990s.
item.subtitle: 1994 / Robert Zemeckis / drama

item.name: Inception
item.reasoning: Won four Academy Awards, grossed over $836 million worldwide, and ranks among IMDb's Top 20 highest-rated films by user vote.
item.subtitle: 2010 / Christopher Nolan / sci-fi

item.name: Interstellar
item.reasoning: Won the Academy Award for Best Visual Effects and grossed over $730 million worldwide, ranking among IMDb's Top 30 films by user rating.
item.subtitle: 2014 / Christopher Nolan / sci-fi

item.name: Fight Club
item.reasoning: Ranks in IMDb's Top 15 films of all time by user rating and grew from a box-office underperformer into one of the most influential cult classics in cinema.
item.subtitle: 1999 / David Fincher / drama

item.name: The Last Airbender
item.reasoning: Holds a 5% critics score on Rotten Tomatoes and won multiple Razzie Awards including Worst Picture and Worst Director for M. Night Shyamalan.
item.subtitle: 2010 / M. Night Shyamalan / fantasy

item.name: Seinfeld
item.reasoning: TV Guide's 2002 list of the 50 Greatest Shows of All Time ranked it #1, and its 1998 finale drew roughly 76 million viewers.
item.subtitle: 1989 NBC sitcom

item.name: Succession
item.reasoning: Won the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series three times (2020, 2022, 2023) and holds a 90s% critics score on Rotten Tomatoes across its run.
item.subtitle: 2018 HBO drama

item.name: Mad Men
item.reasoning: Won the Emmy for Outstanding Drama Series four consecutive years (2008-2011), one of only a handful of shows ever to do so.
item.subtitle: 2007 AMC drama

item.name: The Last of Us
item.reasoning: HBO's 2023 adaptation earned 24 Emmy nominations for its first season and became one of HBO's most-watched series debuts.
item.subtitle: 2023 HBO drama

art.title: The Last of Us Part I

item.name: True Detective
item.reasoning: Its 2014 first season with Matthew McConaughey and Woody Harrelson earned 12 Emmy nominations (winning 5) and was a landmark of prestige TV.
item.subtitle: 2014 HBO anthology

item.name: Friends
item.reasoning: A ratings juggernaut whose 2004 finale drew roughly 52.5 million viewers, though critics rank it below the era's prestige dramas.
item.subtitle: 1994 NBC sitcom

item.name: Cavemen
item.reasoning: Based on GEICO ad characters, it was cancelled during its first season after only a handful of episodes aired and is routinely cited among the worst TV shows ever.
item.subtitle: 2007 ABC sitcom

item.name: Toy Story 3
item.reasoning: Holds a 98% Rotten Tomatoes score, won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature, earned a rare Best Picture nomination, and grossed over $1.06 billion worldwide, widely cited as one of the greatest animated films ever made.
item.subtitle: 2010 / Pixar

item.name: Ratatouille
item.reasoning: Carries a 96% Rotten Tomatoes rating and won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature, consistently ranked among Pixar's finest and most beloved films by critics.
item.subtitle: 2007 / Pixar

item.name: The Incredibles
item.reasoning: Scored 97% on Rotten Tomatoes and won the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature, celebrated as one of the best superhero films of its era.
item.subtitle: 2004 / Pixar

item.name: Soul
item.reasoning: Won both the Academy Award for Best Animated Feature and Best Original Score, holding a 95% Rotten Tomatoes rating as one of Pixar's most acclaimed recent films.
item.subtitle: 2020 / Pixar

item.name: Finding Dory
item.reasoning: Holds a 94% Rotten Tomatoes rating and grossed over $1.02 billion worldwide, a strong and popular sequel though generally rated a notch below Pixar's top-tier originals.
item.subtitle: 2016 / Pixar

item.name: Onward
item.reasoning: Received an 88% Rotten Tomatoes rating but grossed only about $141M worldwide after its theatrical run was cut short by COVID-19 closures, widely regarded as solid but middle-of-the-pack Pixar.
item.subtitle: 2020 / Pixar

item.name: Lightyear
item.reasoning: Was a box-office bomb, grossing roughly $226M against a ~$200M budget for an estimated $106M loss, and holds only a 74% Rotten Tomatoes score, one of Pixar's weakest-received and least profitable major releases.
item.subtitle: 2022 / Pixar

item.name: Pet Sounds
item.subtitle: The Beach Boys, 1966
item.reasoning: Ranked #2 on Rolling Stone's 2020 revised list of the 500 Greatest Albums of All Time, and cited by Paul McCartney as a direct influence on Sgt. Pepper's.

item.name: Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band
item.subtitle: The Beatles, 1967
item.reasoning: Won the 1968 Grammy Award for Album of the Year, the first rock LP ever to do so, and topped Rolling Stone's original 2003 list of the 500 Greatest Albums.

art.term: The Beatles Sgt. Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band

item.name: To Pimp a Butterfly
item.subtitle: Kendrick Lamar, 2015
item.reasoning: Ranked #19 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list of the 500 Greatest Albums of All Time and won the 2016 Grammy for Best Rap Album.

art.term: Kendrick Lamar To Pimp a Butterfly

item.name: The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill
item.subtitle: Lauryn Hill, 1998
item.reasoning: Won the 1999 Grammy Award for Album of the Year, the first hip-hop-rooted album to win the category, and took five Grammys total that night.

art.term: Lauryn Hill The Miseducation of Lauryn Hill

item.name: Blonde on Blonde
item.subtitle: Bob Dylan, 1966
item.reasoning: Ranked #38 on Rolling Stone's 2020 list of the 500 Greatest Albums of All Time and inducted into the Grammy Hall of Fame in 1999.

item.name: Back to Black
item.subtitle: Amy Winehouse, 2006
item.reasoning: Won five Grammy Awards in 2008, including Record and Song of the Year for 'Rehab,' and is one of the UK's best-selling albums of the 21st century.

item.name: Elvis Presley
item.reasoning: The 'King of Rock and Roll' is widely cited as the best-selling solo music artist in history and holds the RIAA record for most gold and platinum certifications by a solo act, having transformed popular music in the 1950s.
item.subtitle: 1954 / rock and roll

item.name: Bob Dylan
item.reasoning: Dylan is the only songwriter awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature (2016) and tops Rolling Stone's greatest-songwriters ranking, reshaping popular music with 'Like a Rolling Stone' and 'Blowin' in the Wind'.
item.subtitle: 1961 / folk rock

item.name: Prince
item.reasoning: A Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductee who won 7 Grammy Awards, an Academy Award for 'Purple Rain', and sold over 100 million records, Prince is routinely ranked among the greatest and most versatile musicians of all time.
item.subtitle: 1978 / funk pop

item.name: Madonna
item.reasoning: The 'Queen of Pop' is recognized by Guinness World Records as the best-selling female recording artist of all time, with over 300 million records sold and the highest-grossing touring female artist in history.
item.subtitle: 1982 / pop

item.name: Stevie Wonder
item.reasoning: Wonder has won 25 competitive Grammy Awards, among the most for any solo artist, and is a Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductee whose 'Songs in the Key of Life' and 'Innervisions' are landmark albums.
item.subtitle: 1963 / soul

item.name: Rihanna
item.reasoning: One of the best-selling music artists of all time with over 250 million records sold, Rihanna holds the RIAA record for the most Diamond-certified singles by any artist and has scored 14 US number-one hits.
item.subtitle: 2005 / pop R&B

item.name: Whataburger
item.subtitle: 1950 / Texas burgers
item.reasoning: Texas-born chain with over 1,000 locations across the South; a cult regional favorite whose orange-and-white A-frame loyalty rivals In-N-Out's, and it regularly places near the top of national fast-food burger rankings.

item.name: Shake Shack
item.subtitle: 2004 / premium burgers
item.reasoning: Danny Meyer's fast-casual chain grew from a Madison Square Park cart into an NYSE-listed company (ticker SHAK) with 500+ locations, and its ShackBurger is frequently rated among America's best fast-food burgers.

item.name: Chipotle
item.subtitle: 1993 / Mexican grill
item.reasoning: Fast-casual leader with over 3,400 restaurants that consistently posts one of the highest ACSI scores among limited-service chains, with a market cap that has topped $80 billion.

item.name: Sonic Drive-In
item.subtitle: 1953 / drive-in
item.reasoning: America's largest drive-in chain with roughly 3,500 locations, beloved for its half-price Happy Hour slushes and cherry limeades but generally rated a solid mid-tier pick in fast-food polls.

item.name: Arby's
item.subtitle: 1964 / roast beef
item.reasoning: Roast-beef specialist with about 3,400 US locations that built a devoted following on its 'We Have the Meats' campaign and Beef 'n Cheddar, landing firmly in the respectable middle of fast-food rankings.

item.name: KFC
item.subtitle: 1952 / fried chicken
item.reasoning: Colonel Sanders' Original Recipe made KFC a global icon with 25,000+ outlets worldwide, but its US reputation and traffic have sagged behind chicken rivals Chick-fil-A and Popeyes, leaving it a middling domestic pick.

item.name: Jack in the Box
item.subtitle: 1951 / burgers, tacos
item.reasoning: West Coast late-night staple with about 2,200 locations known for its cult tacos and all-day menu, but inconsistent quality and a mixed reputation keep it in the lower-middle of consensus rankings.

item.name: Ham
item.reasoning: Ham anchors the Hawaiian pizza (ham and pineapple), one of the world's most recognized pizza varieties, and is a standard topping at Domino's and Pizza Hut worldwide; it consistently ranks among the top ordered meat toppings after pepperoni and sausage.
item.subtitle: Cured pork

item.name: Chicken
item.reasoning: Grilled and BBQ chicken pizzas are top sellers in the US; California Pizza Kitchen's Original BBQ Chicken Pizza is its signature best-selling dish, and chicken ranks as a leading premium topping in US ordering surveys.
item.subtitle: Grilled poultry

item.name: Green Pepper
item.reasoning: Green bell pepper is a standard vegetable topping at virtually every major chain and consistently ranks in the top 10 most-ordered US pizza toppings, typically the leading vegetable alongside onions and mushrooms.
item.subtitle: Bell pepper

item.name: Spinach
item.reasoning: Spinach is a niche topping tied to white and Florentine-style pizzas; national US surveys rank it well below staples like mushrooms, onions, and peppers, appealing mainly to a health-conscious minority.
item.subtitle: Leafy green

item.name: Starburst
item.reasoning: Among the best-selling fruit chews in the US; its soft, juicy taffy squares and the popular FaveREDs (all-red) pack make it a perennial top-tier non-chocolate pick.
item.subtitle: 1960 / Wrigley / fruit chew

item.name: Nerds
item.reasoning: Nerds Gummy Clusters were a breakout hit that helped Ferrara's Nerds surpass $500M in annual sales, making it one of the fastest-growing US candy brands in the 2020s.
item.subtitle: 1983 / Ferrara / tangy crunch

item.name: Butterfinger
item.reasoning: A mainstream staple with a crispy peanut-butter core, reformulated by Ferrara in 2019; well-liked but its brittle, teeth-sticking texture keeps it out of consensus top tiers.
item.subtitle: 1923 / Ferrara / peanut butter

item.name: Milky Way
item.reasoning: A classic Mars bar of caramel and nougat that sells steadily, yet consistently ranks mid-pack behind stablemates Snickers and Twix in candy taste polls.
item.subtitle: 1923 / Mars / caramel nougat

item.name: Almond Joy
item.reasoning: Coconut is famously polarizing, landing this Hershey bar in the lower half of most consensus rankings despite a devoted minority fan base.
item.subtitle: 1946 / Hershey / coconut almond

item.name: Dots
item.reasoning: These Tootsie Roll gumdrops routinely appear on worst-Halloween-candy lists, with a sticky, tooth-pulling texture that draws little enthusiasm in taste rankings.
item.subtitle: 1945 / Tootsie Roll / gumdrops

item.name: Necco Wafers
item.reasoning: Frequently voted the chalkiest/worst candy in America; weak demand contributed to New England Confectionery Company (NECCO) shutting down in 2018.
item.subtitle: 1847 / NECCO / chalky discs

item.name: Canada Dry
item.subtitle: 1904 / ginger ale
item.reasoning: The world's best-selling ginger ale and the default bar mixer for highballs and Moscow mules, a broadly liked, non-polarizing staple.

item.name: Diet Coke
item.subtitle: 1982 / diet cola
item.reasoning: The best-selling diet soft drink in the U.S. for decades; in 2010 it overtook Pepsi to become the No. 2 soft drink in America overall.

item.name: A&W Root Beer
item.subtitle: 1919 / root beer
item.reasoning: An iconic root beer since 1919, but root beer is a polarizing flavor with a narrower fanbase than mainstream colas and lemon-limes.

item.name: 7 Up
item.subtitle: 1929 / lemon-lime
item.reasoning: A classic lemon-lime soda, but it was displaced by Diet Coke as the U.S. No. 3 soft drink in the 1980s and trails Sprite in sales and consensus.

item.name: RC Cola
item.subtitle: 1905 / cola
item.reasoning: The perennial distant 'third cola,' a nostalgic also-ran that has long trailed far behind Coca-Cola and Pepsi in sales and shelf presence.

item.name: Tab
item.subtitle: 1963 / diet cola
item.reasoning: Coca-Cola's first diet cola was rendered obsolete by Diet Coke and discontinued in December 2020, remembered mostly for its harsh saccharin aftertaste.

item.name: Deadpool
item.subtitle: Marvel / 1991
item.reasoning: Deadpool (2016) was the highest-grossing R-rated film at the time with over $780M worldwide, and Deadpool & Wolverine (2024) grossed roughly $1.34 billion, cementing him as a top-tier cultural icon.

item.name: Doctor Strange
item.subtitle: Marvel / 1963
item.reasoning: A central MCU pillar since 2016 with two solo films plus key roles in Avengers: Infinity War and Endgame; Doctor Strange in the Multiverse of Madness (2022) grossed over $955 million worldwide.

item.name: Captain Marvel
item.subtitle: Carol Danvers / Marvel
item.reasoning: Captain Marvel (2019) crossed $1.1 billion worldwide, the first female-led MCU film to hit that milestone, establishing her as a prominent Avenger though a more recent addition to the pantheon.

item.name: The Flash
item.subtitle: Barry Allen / DC
item.reasoning: A founding member of the Justice League and one of DC's most iconic heroes, anchoring The CW's The Flash for nine seasons (2014-2023), the longest-running Arrowverse series.

item.name: Cyclops
item.subtitle: Scott Summers / X-Men
item.reasoning: The original field leader of the X-Men since 1963 and a core team member across comics and films, but rarely a breakout solo star, keeping his general-public profile below the flagship mutants.

item.name: Green Arrow
item.subtitle: Oliver Queen / DC
item.reasoning: Best known to mainstream audiences through The CW's Arrow (2012-2020), which launched the Arrowverse, though he lacks the top-tier film presence and household recognition of DC's core trinity.

item.name: Discord
item.subtitle: 2015 / chat & communities
item.reasoning: Discord surpassed 200 million monthly active users and was valued around $15 billion, becoming the default voice/text platform for gaming and online communities.

item.name: Twitch
item.subtitle: 2011 / live streaming
item.reasoning: Amazon acquired Twitch for about $970 million in 2014, and it remains the dominant live-streaming platform, consistently ranking #1 by hours watched over rivals like YouTube Gaming.

item.name: Tumblr
item.subtitle: 2007 / microblogging
item.reasoning: Yahoo bought Tumblr for $1.1 billion in 2013, but its value collapsed to a roughly $3 million sale to Automattic in 2019 after the adult-content ban, reflecting its decline from peak relevance.

item.name: BeReal
item.subtitle: 2020 / daily photo
item.reasoning: BeReal won Apple's iPhone App of the Year in 2022 and topped app-store charts, but daily active users dropped sharply afterward as the once-a-day novelty faded.

item.name: Vine
item.subtitle: 2013-2017 / short video
item.reasoning: Twitter shut down Vine in 2017; despite pioneering the 6-second looping-video format that shaped TikTok, it failed to monetize or retain creators, making it a defining flameout of the era.

item.name: Myspace
item.subtitle: 2003 / defunct network
item.reasoning: Myspace was the most-visited site in the US in 2006, briefly surpassing Google, but collapsed after Facebook overtook it and infamously lost 12 years of user-uploaded music in a 2019 server migration.

item.name: Border Collie
item.reasoning: Ranked the #1 most intelligent breed in Stanley Coren's 'The Intelligence of Dogs' (learns commands in under 5 repetitions); border collie Chaser was documented understanding 1,022 object names.
item.subtitle: Herding breed

item.name: Rottweiler
item.reasoning: Consistently an AKC top-10 most popular U.S. breed and one of the most widely used police, military, and protection dogs worldwide.
item.subtitle: Working breed

item.name: Australian Shepherd
item.reasoning: Ranks around AKC #12 in U.S. popularity and is a perennial top competitor in agility and flyball, prized for trainability and high work drive.
item.subtitle: Herding breed

item.name: Siberian Husky
item.reasoning: Hugely popular for its wolf-like looks and sled heritage (famed in the 1925 Nome serum run), but widely noted as a demanding escape artist that sheds heavily and needs intense exercise.
item.subtitle: Working sled breed

item.name: Great Dane
item.reasoning: Beloved 'gentle giant' and among the tallest breeds (record-holder Zeus stood 44 inches), but a 7-10 year lifespan and giant-breed health issues temper its ranking.
item.subtitle: Giant working breed

item.name: Shih Tzu
item.reasoning: A long-popular toy companion bred from Tibetan royalty stock, but its brachycephalic face brings breathing and eye problems that keep it a mid-tier pick.
item.subtitle: Toy companion breed

item.name: Pug
item.reasoning: Adored for its comic personality, but its extreme brachycephalic build causes chronic breathing, eye, and overheating problems; UK Royal Veterinary College research led vets to warn against the breed.
item.subtitle: Toy companion breed

item.name: Ferrari
item.reasoning: Brand Finance named Ferrari the world's strongest brand in 2019 with a rare AAA+ rating, and it remains a global benchmark for automotive prestige and desirability.
item.subtitle: Italy / supercars

item.name: Mazda
item.reasoning: Mazda topped Consumer Reports' overall Brand Report Card in 2021 as the highest-scoring brand of all, and consistently ranks among the most reliable mainstream automakers.
item.subtitle: Japan / mainstream

item.name: Audi
item.reasoning: Audi is one of the top-selling luxury marques worldwide, forming the German premium 'Big Three' alongside BMW and Mercedes-Benz.
item.subtitle: Germany / luxury

item.name: Kia
item.reasoning: Kia ranked as the highest-ranked mass-market brand in J.D. Power's 2021 U.S. Initial Quality Study, reflecting a dramatic rise in build quality for a value-focused brand.
item.subtitle: South Korea / value

item.name: Tesla
item.reasoning: Tesla's Model Y and Model 3 are among the best-selling electric cars worldwide, yet Consumer Reports has repeatedly docked the brand for below-average build quality and panel-gap issues.
item.subtitle: USA / electric

item.name: Chevrolet
item.reasoning: Chevrolet is General Motors' highest-volume brand and one of America's best-selling nameplates, but posts only average reliability, landing mid-pack in Consumer Reports rankings.
item.subtitle: USA / mainstream

item.name: Jeep
item.reasoning: Jeep consistently lands near the bottom of Consumer Reports' brand reliability rankings, repeatedly ranking among the least reliable brands surveyed.
item.subtitle: USA / off-road

item.name: Fiat
item.reasoning: Fiat has repeatedly ranked as one of the least dependable brands, finishing at or near dead last in multiple J.D. Power dependability studies and What Car? reliability surveys.
item.subtitle: Italy / low
